entity.label: Gederah
entity.subtitle: City
entity.description: The fortress; a fortified place, a town in the plain (shephelah) of Judah (Josh. 15:36). This is a very common Canaanite and Phoenician name. It is the feminine form of Geder (12:13); the plural form is Gederoth (15:41). This place has by some been identified with Jedireh, a ruin 9 miles from Lydda, toward Eleutheropolis, and 4 miles north of Sur’ah (Zorah), in the valley of Elah.

verses.text: And Sharaim, and Adithaim, and Gederah, and Gederothaim; fourteen cities with their villages:
These were the potters, and those that dwelt among plants and hedges: there they dwelt with the king for his work.
And Ismaiah the Gibeonite, a mighty man among the thirty, and over the thirty; and Jeremiah, and Jahaziel, and Johanan, and Josabad the Gederathite,
